message:
fix: move side effects outside React state updater in createAlert and resolveAlert (#3520) (#3525)

The createAlert and resolveAlert functions were triggering side effects
(notification API calls via sendNotifications, and non-deterministic calls
like generateId/Date.now) inside React state updater callbacks. State
updaters must be pure because React may invoke them multiple times in
Strict Mode or concurrent rendering, causing duplicate notifications.

This commit:
- Moves generateId() and new Date() calls before the setAlerts updater
  so values remain stable across replays
- Moves sendNotifications() calls to a queueMicrotask after the updater,
  ensuring notifications fire exactly once after state commits
- Applies the same fix to resolveAlert which had the same pattern

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
